blocks:
Sūrat al-Mumtaḥinah

*And it was said Sūrat al-Imtiḥān (The Chapter of Testing), and it was said Sūrat al-Mawaddah (The Chapter of Affection). It is Medinan (مدنيّة*). And it consists of thirteen verses by consensus. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَتَّخِذُوا عَدُوِّي وَعَدُوَّكُمْ أَوْلِياءَ (1) O you who have faith! Do not take My enemy and your enemy for allies, Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Ḥāṭib ibn Abī Baltaʿah, and the wording of the verse is general (عامّ) but its meaning is specific (خاصّ). The reason for this was that Ḥāṭib ibn Abī Baltaʿah had embraced Islam and emigrated (هاجر) to Medina, while his family was in Mecca. Quraysh feared that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله would raid them, so they went to Ḥāṭib's family and asked them to write to Ḥāṭib, inquiring about the news of Muḥammad صلى الله عليه وآله and whether He intended to raid Mecca. So they wrote to Ḥāṭib asking him about that, and Ḥāṭib wrote back to them that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله intended that. He handed the letter to a woman named Ṣafiyyah, who placed it in her braids (قرونها) and departed. Then Gabriel (جبرئيل) descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and informed Him of this. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله then sent the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and al-Zubayr ibn al-ʿAwwām in pursuit of her, and they overtook her. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) asked her: "Where is the letter?" She replied: "I have nothing with me." They searched her but found nothing with her. Al-Zubayr said: "We do not see anything with her." The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, we have not lied about the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, nor has the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله lied about Gabriel (جبرئيل), nor has Gabriel (جبرئيل) lied about Allah, exalted is His praise (جلّ ثناؤه). By Allah, if you do not reveal the letter, I will surely send your head back to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." She said: "Move away from me so that I may take it out." So she took the letter out from her braids (قرونها). The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) took it and brought it to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O Ḥāṭib, what is this?" Ḥāṭib replied: "By Allah, O Messenger of Allah, I have neither acted hypocritically (نافقت), nor changed, nor altered [my faith]. I bear witness that there is no god but Allah, and that you are truly the Messenger of Allah. But my family and dependents wrote to me about the good treatment (حُسن صنع) of Quraysh towards them, so I wished to reward Quraysh for their good companionship (حسن معاشرتهم)." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), revealed to His Messenger: يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (O you who have faith) [the rest of] the verse. ** تُلْقُونَ إِلَيْهِمْ بِالْمَوَدَّةِ ** (You convey to them affection,) You disclose affection (المودّة) to them through correspondence (بِالْمُكَاتَبَة). The bāʾ (الباء) is extra (مزيدة). وَقَدْ كَفَرُوا بِما جاءَكُمْ مِنَ الْحَقِّ يُخْرِجُونَ الرَّسُولَ وَإِيَّاكُمْ ** (while they have disbelieved in the truth that has come to you, expelling the Apostle and yourselves,) meaning, from Mecca. أَنْ تُؤْمِنُوا بِاللهِ رَبِّكُمْ ** (because you have faith in Allah, your Lord.) because of your faith (ايمانكم). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ خَرَجْتُمْ ** (If you have gone forth) from your homelands (أوطانكم) جِهاداً فِي سَبِيلِي وَابْتِغاءَ مَرْضاتِي ** (to wage jihad in My way and to seek My pleasure,) The apodosis (جواب الشرط) is omitted, indicated by لا تَتَّخِذُوا (do not take). تُسِرُّونَ إِلَيْهِمْ بِالْمَوَدَّةِ وَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ بِما أَخْفَيْتُمْ وَما أَعْلَنْتُمْ ** (you secretly convey affection to them, while I know best what you hide and what you disclose.) meaning, from among you. Or, aʿlamu (أَعْلَمُ) is a present tense verb (مضارع), and the bāʾ (الباء) is extra (مزيدة). وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْهُ مِنْكُمْ ** (And whoever of you does that,) meaning, performs the act of taking [them as allies] (الاتّخاذ), **فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَواءَ السَّبِيلِ ** (has certainly strayed from the right path.) He has missed it.

(2) ** إِنْ يَثْقَفُوكُمْ ** They gain mastery over you ** يَكُونُوا لَكُمْ أَعْداءً ** and your showing affection to them will not benefit you وَيَبْسُطُوا إِلَيْكُمْ أَيْدِيَهُمْ وَأَلْسِنَتَهُمْ بِالسُّوءِ that which harms you, such as killing and reviling (الشتم) وَوَدُّوا لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ and they desire your apostasy (ارتدادكم). And its coming alone in the past tense form (بلفظ الماضي) is to indicate that they wished that before everything else, and that their wish is established even if they do not gain mastery over you.

(٣) لَنْ تَنْفَعَكُمْ أَرْحامُكُمْ ** (3) Your relatives will not benefit you Your kinship (قرابتكم). وَلا أَوْلادُكُمْ ** nor your children Those for whose sake you show loyalty (توالون) to the polytheists (المشركين). يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ يَفْصِلُ بَيْنَكُمْ ** on the Day of Resurrection. He will separate you He will separate (يفرّق) you due to the terror (الهول) that afflicts you, so some of you will flee from others. What is wrong with you that you reject the right of Allah today for the sake of one who will flee from you tomorrow? And يَفْصِلُ was read (قرئ) with the active voice (على البناء للفاعل) and with tashdid (بالتشديد) in both voices (على البنائين). وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ ** and Allah is watchful of what you do. So He will recompense you for it.

(٤) قَدْ كانَتْ لَكُمْ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ ** (4) Certainly, there is a good exemplar for you The word (قدوة) (exemplar) is a noun for that which is emulated. فِي إِبْراهِيمَ وَالَّذِينَ ** in Abraham and those who (آمنوا) (believed) مَعَهُ إِذْ قالُوا لِقَوْمِهِمْ إِنَّا بُرَآؤُا مِنْكُمْ وَمِمَّا تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ كَفَرْنا بِكُمْ were with him, when they said to their people, 'Indeed we are disavowed of you and whatever you worship besides Allah. We disbelieve in you. means: We disavow ourselves from you (تبرّأنا منكم). This is likewise narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). He said: "And al-kufr (الكفر) in this verse is al-bara'ah (البراءة) (disavowal)." It is narrated in al-Tawhid, and similarly in al-Kafi from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). ** وَبَدا بَيْنَنا وَبَيْنَكُمُ الْعَداوَةُ وَالْبَغْضاءُ أَبَداً حَتَّى تُؤْمِنُوا بِاللهِ وَحْدَهُ ** Enmity and hatred have appeared between us and you forever, until you believe in Allah alone.' Then the enmity (العداوة) and hatred (البغضاء) will turn into affection (ألفة) and love (محبّة). إِلَّا قَوْلَ إِبْراهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ لَأَسْتَغْفِرَنَّ لَكَ ** (Except for Abraham's saying to his father, 'I will surely plead forgiveness for you, This is an exception (استثناء) from His saying: أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ (a good exemplar), for his seeking forgiveness (استغفاره) for his disbelieving father is not among that which you ought to emulate (تأتسوا به). For indeed, it was due to a promise he had made to him, as preceded in Surat al-Tawbah (سورة التوبة). وَما أَمْلِكُ لَكَ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** and I cannot avail you anything against Allah.') This is part of the completion of the exempted saying (قوله المستثنى). The exception of the whole does not necessitate the exception of all its parts. رَبَّنا عَلَيْكَ تَوَكَّلْنا وَإِلَيْكَ أَنَبْنا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ ** Our Lord! In You do we trust, and to You do we turn penitently, and toward You is the ultimate return. This is connected to what preceded the exception (الاستثناء).

(٥)** رَبَّنا لا تَجْعَلْنا فِتْنَةً لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (5) Our Lord! Do not make us a trial for the faithless, by giving them dominance over us, so they tempt us with a punishment we cannot bear, or by making them rejoice at our misfortune.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: There was no believer among the children of Adam except that he was poor, nor any disbeliever except that he was rich, until Ibrahim (peace be upon him) came and said: رَبَّنا لا تَجْعَلْنا فِتْنَةً لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ** (Our Lord! Do not make us a trial for the faithless,) So Allah placed wealth and need among these (believers) and wealth and need among those (disbelievers). ** وَاغْفِرْ لَنا ** (and forgive us) what has slipped from us. رَبَّنا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ (Our Lord! Indeed You are the All-mighty, the All-wise.) And whoever is thus, He is worthy of strengthening the one who relies upon Him (المتوكّل) and answering the one who calls upon Him (الدّاعي).

**(٦) لَقَدْ كانَ لَكُمْ فِيهِمْ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ ** (6) There is certainly a good exemplar for you in them,

A repetition for the purpose of further urging the emulation (التأسّي) of them, specifically through Abraham (بِإِبْرَاهِيمَ). And for that reason, it was introduced with an oath (بالقسم) and confirmed by what follows it: لِمَنْ كانَ يَرْجُوا اللهَ وَالْيَوْمَ الْآخِرَ (for those who look forward to Allah and the Last Day). So, realize that abandoning the emulation (التأسّي) of them indicates a deficiency (سوء) in belief (العقيدة). وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّ فَإِنَّ اللهَ هُوَ الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ. And whoever turns away—Allah is indeed the All-sufficient, the All-laudable.

(٧) عَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَجْعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ عادَيْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ مَوَدَّةً وَاللهُ قَدِيرٌ ** (7) Maybe Allah will bring about affection between you and those of them whom you have taken as enemies, and Allah is all-powerful. [He is] capable على ذلك (on that). وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. [He is All-forgiving, All-merciful] لما فرط منكم من موالاتهم من قبل (for what preceded from you of befriending them before) ولما بقي في قلوبكم من ميل الرحم (and for what remained in your hearts of the inclination of kinship).

Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: Indeed, Allah commanded His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the believers to disavow their people as long as they were disbelievers. So He said: لَقَدْ كانَ لَكُمْ فِيهِمْ أُسْوَةٌ حَسَنَةٌ ** (Indeed, there is a good example for you in them) up to His saying: وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (And Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful). Allah severed the guardianship (ولاية) of the believers from them, and they showed them enmity. Then He said: عَسَى اللهُ أَنْ يَجْعَلَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَبَيْنَ الَّذِينَ عادَيْتُمْ مِنْهُمْ مَوَدَّةً ** (Maybe Allah will bring about affection between you and those of them whom you have taken as enemies). So when the people of Mecca embraced Islam, the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم mixed with them and intermarried with them, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم married Umm Habiba, the daughter of Abu Sufyan ibn Harb.

(٨) ** لا يَنْهاكُمُ اللهُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ لَمْ يُقاتِلُوكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ وَلَمْ يُخْرِجُوكُمْ مِنْ دِيارِكُمْ أَنْ تَبَرُّوهُمْ وَتُقْسِطُوا إِلَيْهِمْ ** (8) Allah does not forbid you from those who did not fight you because of religion and did not expel you from your homes, that you should be kind to them and act justly towards them.

that you judge justly towards them (بالعدل) ** إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُقْسِطِينَ ** the equitable ones (العادلين).

It was narrated: That Qutaylah bint ʿAbd al-ʿUzzā came, while she was a polytheist (مشركة), to her daughter Asmā’ bint Abī Bakr with gifts, but she did not accept them, nor did she permit her to enter. So, [the verse] was revealed.

(٩) إِنَّما يَنْهاكُمُ اللهُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ قاتَلُوكُمْ فِي الدِّينِ وَأَخْرَجُوكُمْ مِنْ دِيارِكُمْ وَظاهَرُوا عَلى إِخْراجِكُمْ ** (9) Allah forbids you only from those who fought you because of [your] religion, and expelled you from your homes, and backed [others] in your expulsion, like the polytheists of Mecca (مكّة), for some of them strove in expelling the believers, and some of them aided those who expelled [them]. أَنْ تَوَلَّوْهُمْ وَمَنْ يَتَوَلَّهُمْ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ ** (9) that you make friends with them. And whoever makes friends with them—it is they who are the wrongdoers. because they place the friendship (الولاية) in other than its proper place.

(١٠) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا جاءَكُمُ الْمُؤْمِناتُ مُهاجِراتٍ فَامْتَحِنُوهُنَ ** (O you who have faith! When faithful women come to you as emigrants, test them.) So test them with what makes it most probable in your estimation that their hearts agree with their tongues regarding faith (الإِيمان). اللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِإِيمانِهِنَ for He is the One Who is aware of what is in their hearts. فَإِنْ عَلِمْتُمُوهُنَّ مُؤْمِناتٍ by their oath and the appearance of the signs (الامارات), فَلا تَرْجِعُوهُنَّ إِلَى الْكُفَّارِ to their disbelieving husbands. لا هُنَّ حِلٌّ لَهُمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحِلُّونَ لَهُنَ The repetition is for emphasis and correspondence, or the first [negation] is for the occurrence of separation, and the second is for the prohibition of renewal [of the marriage]. وَآتُوهُمْ ما أَنْفَقُوا what they spent on them of the dowries (المهور).

Al-Qummi said: If a woman from the polytheists joins the Muslims, she is tested by making her swear by Allah that what led her to join the Muslims was neither hatred for her disbelieving husband nor love for any Muslim, but rather it was Islam that led her to do so. If she swears to that, her Islam is accepted. وَآتُوهُمْ ما أَنْفَقُوا ** (And give them what they spent) means that the Muslim woman returns her dowry (صداقها) to her disbelieving husband, and then a Muslim man marries her.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was said to Him: "My wife has a sister who is knowledgeable (عارفة) and holds our view (رأينا) with insight (بالبصيرة), but [she is not married]. Should I marry her to someone who does not hold her view?" He said: "No, by no means! Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: فَلا تَرْجِعُوهُنَّ إِلَى الْكُفَّارِ لا هُنَّ حِلٌّ لَهُمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحِلُّونَ لَهُنَ وَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْ تَنْكِحُوهُنَ ** (Do not return them to the disbelievers. Neither are they lawful for them, nor are they lawful for them, and there is no sin upon you in marrying them)." For Islam has intervened between them and their disbelieving husbands: إِذا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَ ** (when you give them their dowries). In this, there is an indication that what was given to their [former] husbands does not take the place of the dowry (المهر). وَلا تُمْسِكُوا (1) بِعِصَمِ الْكَوافِرِ ** (And do not hold on to the ties of disbelieving women) by which the disbelieving women are bound, such as the contract (عقد) and lineage (نسب). (عصمة) is the plural of (عصمة). The intent is the prohibition for the believers from remaining married to polytheist women. And it was recited with the doubling (تشديد) of the sin (السين).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse. He said: He (Allah) says: Whoever has a disbelieving wife—meaning, one who is not upon the religion (ملّة) of Islam, while he is upon the religion (ملّة) of Islam—let him offer Islam to her. If she accepts, she is his wife; otherwise, she is free from him. Thus, Allah forbade holding on to her marital tie (عصمتها).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: It is not proper to marry the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب). It was said: "And where is its prohibition?" He said: His saying: وَلا تُمْسِكُوا بِعِصَمِ الْكَوافِرِ. (And do not hold on to the ties of disbelieving women.)

I say: What contradicts this has already passed in Surat al-Ma'idah. وَسْئَلُوا ما أَنْفَقْتُمْ ** (And ask for what you have spent) of the dowries (مهور) of your wives who joined the disbelievers. وَلْيَسْئَلُوا ما أَنْفَقُوا ** (and let them ask for what they have spent) of the dowries (مهور) of their wives who emigrated. ذلِكُمْ حُكْمُ اللهِ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ ** (That is Allah’s judgment, which He judges between you, and Allah is All-knowing, All-wise.) He legislates what His wisdom (حكمته) necessitates.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means: وَإِنْ فاتَكُمْ شَيْءٌ مِنْ أَزْواجِكُمْ ** (And if any of your wives escape you) and join the disbelievers from among the people of your covenant (أهل عهدكم), then ask them for her dowry (صداقها). And if any of their women join you, then give them her dowry (صداقها). ** ذلِكُمْ حُكْمُ اللهِ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ. (That is Allah’s judgment, which He judges between you.)

footnote (١) Meaning: Do not hold on to the marriage (*نكاح*) of disbelieving women. The root meaning of *al-ʿiṣmah* (*العصمة*) is prevention (*المنع*). Marriage (*النكاح*) is called *ʿiṣmah* because the woman who is married is under the protection (*حبال*) and safeguard (*عصمته*) of the husband.

(١١) وَإِنْ فاتَكُمْ شَيْءٌ مِنْ أَزْواجِكُمْ إِلَى الْكُفَّارِ ** (11) And if any of your wives escape to the faithless meaning, they preceded you and escaped from you to them, فَعاقَبْتُمْ (then you retaliated). It was said: meaning, your turn (عقبتكم) came, that is, your turn (نوبتكم) to pay the dowry (المهر). I say: Rather, the meaning is that you married another woman immediately after her (عقيبها), as its explanation will follow. فَآتُوا ** O believers الَّذِينَ ذَهَبَتْ أَزْواجُهُمْ مِثْلَ ما أَنْفَقُوا ** then give those whose wives have gone away the like of what they have spent. Al-Qummi says: And if they joined the disbelievers with whom there is no treaty between you, and you obtained spoils (غنيمة), فَآتُوا الَّذِينَ ذَهَبَتْ أَزْواجُهُمْ مِثْلَ ما أَنْفَقُوا. then give those whose wives have gone away the like of what they have spent. I say: It is as if he made the meaning of فَعاقَبْتُمْ ** (then you retaliated) to be: you obtained from the disbelievers a consequence (عقبى), meaning spoils (غنيمة). That is, give them from the spoils (الغنيمة) as a replacement for what was lost. He said: And he said the reason for the revelation of that [verse] is that Fatimah bint Abi Umayyah ibn al-Mughirah was married to Umar ibn al-Khattab, but she disliked migrating with him and remained with the polytheists. Then Mu'awiyah ibn Abi Sufyan married her. So Allah commanded His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) to give Umar the like of her dowry (صداقها).

In al-Ilal, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): They were asked about the meaning of al-uqubah (العقوبة) here. He said: Indeed, the one whose wife has gone away retaliated (عاقب) by marrying another woman besides her, meaning he married her. So, when he marries another woman besides her, it is incumbent upon the Imam (الإِمام) to give him the dowry (مهر) of his departed wife. Then they were asked: How is it that the believers return the dowry (المهر) to her husband without any action on their part in her departure, and that the believers must return to her husband what he spent on her from what the believers obtain? He said: The Imam (الإمام) returns it to him, whether they obtained [spoils] from the disbelievers or did not obtain [them], because it is incumbent upon the Imam (الإِمام) to secure his need from what is under his authority (تحت يده). And if the division (القسمة) is present, he has the right to cover every calamity (نائبة) that befalls him before the division. If anything remains after that, he divides it among them, and if nothing remains, then there is nothing for them.

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), the like of it, except that he said: It is incumbent upon the Imam (الإِمام) to permit a group (جماعة) from what is under his authority (تحت يده).

In al-Jawami': When the preceding verse was revealed, the believers paid what they were commanded regarding the expenditures (نفقات) of the polytheists on their wives. But the polytheists refused to return anything of the dowries (مهور) of the disbelieving women to their Muslim husbands. So, the following was revealed: ** وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ الَّذِي أَنْتُمْ بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ ** and be wary of Allah, in whom you are believers. for belief in Him is what necessitates being wary of Him (التقوى منه).

(١٢) يا أَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذا جاءَكَ الْمُؤْمِناتُ يُبايِعْنَكَ عَلى أَنْ لا يُشْرِكْنَ بِاللهِ شَيْئاً وَلا يَسْرِقْنَ وَلا يَزْنِينَ وَلا يَقْتُلْنَ أَوْلادَهُنَ ** (12) O Prophet! When the faithful women come to you to pledge allegiance to you, that they will not ascribe any partners to Allah, and will not steal, nor commit adultery, nor kill their children, meaning the burying alive of daughters (وأد البنات) or abortion (الاسقاط). وَلا يَأْتِينَ بِبُهْتانٍ يَفْتَرِينَهُ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِنَّ وَأَرْجُلِهِنَ ** nor bring any calumny that they may fabricate between their hands and feet, In al-Jawami': A woman used to pick up a newborn and say to her husband, "This is my child from you." The calumny (البهتان) fabricated between her hands and feet is an allusion (كنّى) to the child she falsely attributes (تلصقه) to her husband, because her womb, in which she carries the child, is between the hands, and her private part (فرجها), through which she gives birth, is between the feet. وَلا يَعْصِينَكَ فِي مَعْرُوفٍ ** nor disobey you in any good matter, concerning a good deed (حسنة) with which you command them. Al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is what Allah has made obligatory upon them, such as prayer (الصلاة) and Zakat (الزكاة), and whatever good He commands them with. ** فَبايِعْهُنَ وَاسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُنَّ اللهَ إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ. then accept their allegiance and plead with Allah for their forgiveness. Indeed, Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم conquered Mecca, he accepted the allegiance of the men. Then the women came to pledge allegiance to him, so Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down O Prophet! (the Ayah). Hind said: "As for the children, we raised them when they were small, and you killed them when they were grown." And Umm al-Hakam bint al-Harith ibn Hisham, who was married to Ikrimah ibn Abi Jahl, said: "O Messenger of Allah, what is that good matter (المعروف) concerning which Allah commanded us not to disobey you?" He said: "Do not slap a cheek (خدّاً), nor scratch a face (وجهاً), nor pluck hair (شعراً), nor tear a garment (جيباً), nor blacken a garment (ثوباً), nor call out 'Woe!' (بويل)." So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم accepted their allegiance upon this. She asked: "O Messenger of Allah, how shall we pledge allegiance to you?" He said: "Indeed, I do not shake hands with women." So, He called for a vessel (يقدح) of water, put His hand into it, and then withdrew it. He said: "Put your hands into this water; that is the allegiance (البيعة)." And al-Qummi mentioned Abd al-Muttalib instead of Hisham, and added: "Nor stand at a grave (قبر)."

And in another narration in al-Kafi: "Nor spread hair (شعراً)."

And in it (al-Kafi), narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: He gathered them around Him, then called for a bronze basin (تور برام), poured pure water (ماء نضوحا) into it, then dipped His hand into it. Then He said: "Listen, O you women! I accept your allegiance upon the condition that you will not ascribe any partners to Allah, nor steal, nor commit adultery, nor kill your children, nor bring any calumny that you fabricate between your hands and feet, nor disobey your husbands (بعولتكنّ) in any good matter. Have you affirmed this?" They said: "Yes." Then He withdrew His hand from the basin (التور) and said to them: "Dip your hands." So they did. And the pure hand of the Messenger of Allah was too noble (أطيب) to touch the palm of a female who was not His mahram (بمحرم).

(١٣) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تَتَوَلَّوْا قَوْماً غَضِبَ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (13) O you who have faith! Do not take as friends a people with whom Allah is wrathful. This means the generality of the disbelievers (الكفّار) or the Jews, since it is narrated that it was revealed concerning some of the poor Muslims who used to maintain relations with the Jews in order to obtain some of their fruits. قَدْ يَئِسُوا مِنَ الْآخِرَةِ ** (They have despaired of the Hereafter) because of their disbelief in it, or because of their knowledge that there is no share for them in it due to their obstinacy toward the Messenger, who is described in the Torah and supported by miracles (المعجزات). كَما يَئِسَ الْكُفَّارُ مِنْ أَصْحابِ الْقُبُورِ ** (just as the faithless have despaired of the occupants of the graves) that they will be resurrected, or rewarded, or that any good will reach them from them (the occupants of the graves), or كَما يَئِسَ الْكُفَّارُ ** (just as the faithless have despaired) who have died and witnessed the Hereafter.

( A Narrative Discussion )

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surat al-Mumtahanah in his obligatory prayers (فرائضه) and supererogatory prayers (نوافله), Allah will test His heart for faith (الإِيمان), illuminate his sight, and poverty will never afflict him, nor will madness afflict his body or his child, if Allah the Almighty wills."
